originalTitle: Astonished Belgium vow to ‘defend football’ after Fifa’s shock Balogun reversal
summary: Belgium coach Rudi Garcia likened Fifa’s decision to an April Fools’ Day joke as the national federation said it would ‘investigate all potential options’
content: As a Fifa media officer read aloud the statement confirming the governing body’s shock reversal of US striker Folarin Balogun’s suspension on Sunday, Belgium coach Rudi Garcia and goalkeeper Thibaut Courtois put their poker faces to work. Garcia stared straight down the aisle of the press conference room at Seattle Stadium. Courtois’s eyes fluttered about, perhaps masking some rolls as he faced a press pack eager to make sense of an unexpected World Cup twist. Balogun’s reinstatement came across as a joke to the Belgian boss, though he hardly seemed ready for a laugh. “I didn’t know that 5 July was equal to 1 April [April Fools’ Day] at Fifa,” Garcia said in his native French. “I think we should refer to the [statement] of my federation, the Belgian federation. I think a lot of things are in it. The federation does not defend itself, it does not defend the national team – it defends football in general. It defends its integrity. It defends its ethics.” In that statement released Sunday, the Belgian federation dove deeper into the disciplinary code in response to Fifa’s citation of Article 27 to justify the change in Balogun’s punishment. Fifa had previously used Article 27 to clear Cristiano Ronaldo to start Portugal’s opening World Cup game after he received a red card in a November qualifier. “The Royal Belgian Football Association [RBFA] is astonished by Fifa’s decision to declare suspended United States player Folarin Balogun eligible to play,” it said. “In order to safeguard the legitimate rights of all participating teams and to protect the fundamental principles of fair play in our sport, both at this FIFA World Cup and at future editions of the tournament, the RBFA is investigating all potential options.” When asked if it was fair to Belgium’s preparations to make such a change on the eve of a knockout match, Courtois demurred. “That’s a question for the football world, not for me as a player,” the goalkeeper said. “Of course it surprises you a bit, especially because it’s the day before the game. If the day after the game it’s already decided, you can adjust to it. But as a player group, we have to win the game on the field. He’s a good player, but we have to win against the whole of America. For us as a group of players, we are going to do our thing on the field. We deserve it with our football and we want to win.” As word of the suspension of Balogun’s ban began to spread, Norway head coach Ståle Solbakken became the first to comment on it, doing so after his team’s 2-0 win over Brazil in the last 16. “It is a big mistake by Fifa. It is not a great conclusion. He got a red card and the VAR concluded that it was a red card. That means you are suspended for one game,” he said. “I think what is really bad about that situation is, if the USA beat Belgium, they will always have that extra thing about it. The Belgians will be furious. What about the next red card? What happens there? Will some committee take it away? It’s a bad, bad, bad, bad decision that will hurt the World Cup and the United States.”
chineseTitle: 国际足联令人震惊的巴洛贡逆转后，比利时发誓要“捍卫足球”
chineseSummary: 比利时教练鲁迪·加西亚将国际足联的决定比作愚人节笑话，因为国家足协表示将“调查所有潜在的选择”
chineseContent: 当一名国际足联媒体官员大声宣读了一份声明，证实管理机构周日对美国前锋福拉林·巴洛贡的禁赛决定令人震惊地逆转时，比利时教练鲁迪·加西亚和门将蒂博·库尔图瓦都摆出一副扑克脸。加西亚直视着西雅图体育场新闻发布会室的过道。库尔图瓦的眼睛四处飘动，也许是为了掩盖一些滚动，因为他面对着一群渴望了解世界杯意外转折的记者。巴洛贡的复职对这位比利时主帅来说只是一个笑话，尽管他似乎还没有准备好笑。 “我不知道 7 月 5 日等于国际足联的 4 月 1 日（愚人节），”加西亚用他的母语法语说道。 “我认为我们应该参考比利时足协的[声明]。我认为其中包含了很多内容。足协不捍卫自己，也不捍卫国家队——它捍卫整个足球运动。它捍卫其完整性。它捍卫其道德。”在周日发布的声明中，比利时足协更深入地探讨了纪律守则，以回应国际足联援引第 27 条来证明改变巴洛贡处罚的合理性。此前，国际足联曾根据第27条规定，在克里斯蒂亚诺·罗纳尔多(Cristiano Ronaldo)在11月预选赛中吃到红牌后，他可以在葡萄牙世界杯首场比赛中首发。声明称：“比利时皇家足球协会 (RBFA) 对国际足联决定宣布被禁赛的美国球员弗拉林·巴洛贡有资格参加比赛感到惊讶。” “为了保障所有参赛球队的合法权利，并保护我们这项运动公平竞争的基本原则，无论是在本届世界杯还是在未来的赛事中，RBFA 正在调查所有可能的选择。”当被问及比利时的准备工作是否公平时
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
